error_signature: Reference error: Capitalize is not defined
title: Capitalize function not defined in JavaScript code
symptom: The code throws a reference error indicating that the 'capitalize' function is not defined, preventing execution.
applies_when: The function is not defined in the code
not_this_if: The function is defined elsewhere in the code
root_cause: The error occurs because the JavaScript interpreter cannot find a definition for the 'capitalize' function when it is called. This typically happens when the function is either not declared or is declared after its usage in the code.
fix_steps: Define the 'capitalize' function before it is called in the code.
Insert the following code: function capitalize(txt) { return txt.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + txt.substr(1).toLowerCase(); }
agent_instruction: Define the 'capitalize' function before its usage in your JavaScript code.
